summary: DINE Pillar OS-Awareness testing complete. All 19 backend tests passed. DINE pillar correctly handles home nutrition queries (meal plan, kibble/wet food, portions, diet transitions). os_context properly generates dine_context, dine_picks, safety_gates with allergies, temporal_context for birthdays, and picks_update signals. Non-DINE queries (celebrate, care, travel, fit) route correctly.

minor.issue: 'treats for training' query routes to CELEBRATE instead of DINE due to 'treats' keyword overlap. Not blocking - by design per pillar keyword scoring.

dine_pillar_detection.diet_transition_query: PASS - routes to pillar='dine' with diet_transition pick

dine_context_generation.structure: PASS - fields: current_diet, allergies, diet_restrictions, weight_status, age_band, activity_level

dine_picks.allergy_consult_pick: PASS - nutrition_consult pick for pet with allergies

os_context_verified_fields.dine_context: VERIFIED - returns {current_diet, allergies, diet_restrictions, weight_status, age_band, activity_level}
os_context_verified_fields.dine_picks: VERIFIED - returns Concierge Pick Cards with title, cta, service_type

critical_code_review_comments: PILLARS['dine']['keywords'] correctly includes nutrition keywords: meal plan, kibble, wet food, portion, how much to feed, etc.
get_mira_os_context() properly generates dine_context and dine_picks for DINE pillar
Line 8758-8762 correctly keeps nutrition queries in DINE pillar (no longer switches to FIT)
detect_pillar() uses keyword scoring - higher score wins when keywords overlap between pillars
seed_data_creation: None - used existing Mojo pet data
context_for_next_testing_agent: DINE pillar OS-awareness fully verified. All nutrition queries (meal plan, kibble, wet food, portions, diet transitions) correctly route to DINE pillar. os_context returns dine_context with allergies, dine_picks with Concierge Pick Cards, safety_gates with Chicken allergy, temporal_context with birthday (2 days away). Non-DINE pillars (celebrate, care, travel, fit) continue to work correctly. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_dine_pillar_os_awareness.py can be reused for regression testing.
